mutex_groups.contract-*: Only one slice at a time may touch src/lib/contracts/**
mutex_groups.scanEngine-decompose: Only one P1.A0.* slice at a time — all touch scanEngine.ts
mutex_groups.migration-*: Only one DB migration at a time

slices.title: Promote engine PipelineResult to VerdictBlock zod schema
dod: src/lib/contracts/verdict.ts exports VerdictBlockSchema (zod) and VerdictBlock type via z.infer
Schema covers every field of PipelineResult + AgentOutput + MatchPrediction from src/lib/engine/types.ts
Round-trip parse test: sample PipelineResult → JSON.stringify → JSON.parse → schema.parse → deep equal
npm run check exits 0
src/lib/contracts/index.ts re-exports VerdictBlockSchema and VerdictBlock
slices.notes: The ground truth for verdict_block shape already exists as TypeScript interfaces in src/lib/engine/types.ts. This is a 'promote existing interface to zod' task, not greenfield design. Do not widen or narrow field types without explicit comment.

slices.title: Promote DecisionTrajectory schema to zod
dod: src/lib/contracts/trajectory.ts exports DecisionTrajectorySchema (zod) and DecisionTrajectory type
Schema embeds VerdictBlock via VerdictBlockSchema (from P0.1)
Outcome fields are nullable (resolver populates later)
Round-trip parse test covers both unresolved and resolved trajectories
npm run check exits 0
src/lib/contracts/index.ts re-exports DecisionTrajectorySchema and DecisionTrajectory
slices.notes: Schema basis is ORPO_DATA_SCHEMA_PIPELINE_v1 §2. Embeds P0.1's VerdictBlock as a JSONB payload in DB terms. Outcome fields start nullable because the time-delay resolver worker (P2.C-outcome-resolver, later slice) populates them.

slices.title: Finalize contract ID namespace — close RawId registry + add verdict/trajectory/pair IDs
dod: VerdictBias, VerdictUrgency, StructureStateId, EventDirection, EventSeverity enums exported from ids.ts
RawId remains open (template literal), isRawId() function unchanged
Barrel re-export updated if new enums need re-export via index.ts
npm run check exits 0
slices.notes: Partially drafted already on feat/phase0-contracts. This slice finishes the enum additions that verdict.ts and trajectory.ts depend on. Can run in parallel with P0.1/P0.2 only if mutex is per-file instead of directory-wide — default setting uses directory-wide so this runs before P0.1.

slices.title: Contracts barrel re-export finalization + drop-in type replacement verification
dod: src/lib/contracts/index.ts re-exports all public schemas and types from verdict, trajectory, ids
One representative call site in src/lib/engine/types.ts replaced: interface PipelineResult becomes type PipelineResult = z.infer<typeof VerdictBlockSchema>
agentPipeline.ts continues to compile and run existing callers without code changes
npm run check exits 0
Regression test: quickPipeline output still parses via VerdictBlockSchema without errors
slices.notes: This is the verification gate for Phase 0. If this slice passes, contracts are effectively frozen and Phase 1 unblocks. Any P1.* slice may then depend only on the contracts layer, not on engine/types.ts directly.

slices.title: Pin zod as explicit dependency in package.json
dod: zod explicitly listed in dependencies (not just transitive via @walletconnect)
Version ≥ 3.25 and pinned (no caret or tilde)
package-lock.json regenerated via npm install
npm run check exits 0
No other dependency changes
slices.notes: Prevents zod from silently disappearing if @walletconnect drops it. Smallest possible slice — should merge in under an hour. Safe to run in parallel with P0.1–P0.4 (different file scope).

slices.title: Extract binance raw loader to src/lib/server/providers/binance.ts
dod: src/lib/server/providers/binance.ts created with raw loader functions moved out of scanEngine.ts (Phase A plan first, Phase B execute)
All binance fetch logic previously in scanEngine.ts is now in providers/binance.ts
scanEngine.ts re-exports binance loaders via barrel for backward compatibility
No behavior changes, no function signature changes, no type widening
npm run check exits 0
All existing imports of binance functions resolve unchanged
slices.notes: Decomposer agent runs in two phases. Phase A produces a plan at .agent-context/handoffs/P1.A0-binance-split-plan.md and STOPS for human approval. Phase B executes the move as one PR. This is the first decomposer invocation and will validate the pattern for the other P1.A0.* slices.

slices.title: Extract coingecko raw loader to src/lib/server/providers/coingecko.ts
dod: src/lib/server/providers/coingecko.ts created, coingecko fetches moved out of scanEngine.ts
scanEngine.ts re-exports coingecko loaders via barrel
No behavior changes
npm run check exits 0
binance extraction from P1.A0-binance remains intact
slices.notes: Depends on P1.A0-binance MERGED (not just started) because both touch scanEngine.ts. Mutex enforces this at the scheduler level. Pattern should now be established from binance; coingecko is the pattern repeat.

slices.title: Extract coinalyze raw loader to src/lib/server/providers/coinalyze.ts
dod: src/lib/server/providers/coinalyze.ts created, coinalyze fetches moved out of scanEngine.ts
scanEngine.ts re-exports coinalyze loaders via barrel
No behavior changes
npm run check exits 0
slices.notes: Serial via mutex after P1.A0-coingecko. By the third P1.A0.* slice the decomposer pattern is fully proven and any issues would have surfaced earlier.

slices.title: Extract dexscreener raw loader to src/lib/server/providers/dexscreener.ts
dod: src/lib/server/providers/dexscreener.ts created, dexscreener fetches moved out of scanEngine.ts
scanEngine.ts re-exports dexscreener loaders via barrel
No behavior changes
npm run check exits 0
slices.notes: Last of the v0 provider extractions. After this, Phase 1 A-P0 has 4 of the 21 providers extracted; the remaining 17 are added to the DAG in a v1 update after first round merges cleanly.

slices.title: Add verdict_block_json JSONB column to decision_trajectories + round-trip test
dod: Migration file creates decision_trajectories table if not exists (with verdict_block_json JSONB column) OR adds the column if the table already exists
Migration includes both up and down paths
src/lib/server/memory/decisionJournal.ts exports insertTrajectory() and getTrajectory() using DecisionTrajectorySchema for validation
Round-trip integration test: insertTrajectory(sample) → getTrajectory(id) → schema.parse → deep equal sample
npm run check exits 0
Supabase RLS policy drafted for trajectory table (even if permissive for now)
slices.notes: C-schema track per three-pipeline Phase 1. Runs in parallel with P1.A0.* (different mutex group). This slice defines the first real consumer of the DecisionTrajectorySchema from P0.2 and provides the database surface for the later outcome-resolver worker (future slice, not in this v0 DAG).

parking_lot: P1.A0-etherscan, P1.A0-santiment, P1.A0-lunarcrush, P1.A0-defillama, P1.A0-cryptoquant, P1.A0-glassnode, P1.A0-yahoo, P1.A0-fred, P1.A0-feargreed, P1.A0-coinmetrics, P1.A0-coinmarketcap, P1.A0-dune, P1.A0-gmx, P1.A0-polymarket, P1.A0-geckowhale (16 remaining providers from the 21 atoms — added to DAG v1 after v0 proves the pattern)
P2.A1-events (rewrite F4~F17 as event emitters) — Phase 2 work
P2.C-outcome-resolver (time-bridge worker that populates trajectory outcomes) — Phase 2 work

notes.v0_scope: First 10 slices covering Phase 0 (contract freeze, 5 slices) + Phase 1 first half (4 provider extractions + 1 DB migration, 5 slices). This is enough to unblock the entire trunk plan without committing to slice definitions that have not been validated yet.
notes.v1_trigger: When P0.4-contracts-barrel and P1.A0-binance both merge successfully, add the remaining 16 providers and Phase 2 slices.
notes.ordering_rationale: P0.1 and P0.2 are mutex-serialized (contract-*), so even though they could in theory run in parallel with different target files, default setting runs them serial for safety. P0.5-zod-pin has no mutex and can run in parallel. P0.3 can run first since P0.1 and P0.2 depend on the enums it finalizes.
notes.priority_explanation: Priority breaks ties when multiple slices are ready at the same scheduler tick. P0.1 (100) is the highest because contract freeze is the entire critical path.
notes.research_track_absent_from_v0: v0 is product-track only. Research track (research-scientist, data-formatter, eval-runner) comes online at Phase 2 rollout (swarm-v1 week 2). First research slice will be 'baseline hit_rate measurement on FIXED_SCENARIOS' and depends on P0.2-trajectory-zod being merged.
